examples: Opening description: 'No one else would have been allowed...' softened to Jaina's visible inference so the room still belongs to her eye.
Interrogation explanation: 'It is preserving itself through transit' revised to plainer Jaina field speech without flattening her intelligence.
Sylvanas line: 'surviving the experiment' changed to 'surviving the road' to keep her dry voice concrete and spoken.
Humor beat: 'took a heartbeat to register as humor' revised so the line reads as Jaina hearing the joke, not the narrator diagnosing it.
Border-exit beat: 'its absence hit her like open air where a wall should have been' tightened to a cleaner exposed-state image.
Escort read: 'Jaina noticed it only because...' trimmed to reduce filter-distance and keep the sentence in scene.
Post-carcass silence: abstract 'working silence' revised to a more immediate Jaina reading of how the others take the answer.
observations: Opening cadence matches the prior chapter's clipped handoff and keeps the chapter in immediate friction.
Jaina's early speech still needs one visible technical slip; that slip now stays load-bearing instead of spreading across later dialogue.
Sylvanas's wit works best when the nouns stay physical: road, saddle, arrow, horse, not abstract framing words.
Paragraph shapes stay mostly within the 1-4 sentence target from the style guide, with intentional clipped bursts in interrogation and action.
The chapter's best voice moments are the deadpan exchanges where each woman refuses to soften for the other.

examples: Opening rank inference trimmed so Jaina reads authority from what she sees instead of stating institutional permission as fact.
Mid-road embarrassment beat changed from 'Jaina felt the moment she had misstepped' to a more direct interior phrasing.
Deer aftermath honesty beat revised from meta-awareness into a simpler Jaina perception-response line.
observations: I checked the opening room description, the escort-formation read, the Millhaven theory correction, the wildlife fight, and the carcass diagnosis for narrator intrusion.
Sylvanas remains readable only through what Jaina sees: clipped questions, speed of command, tactical positioning, and dry replies.
No omniscient future knowledge was added at the chapter seam; the opening still stays inside the same moment from chapter1.
Cyndia, Lyriel, and Neral are observed as working bodies in space, not explained from outside Jaina's frame.
The chapter still lets Jaina misread, revise, and self-correct in real time, which is the right epistemology for limited third.

notes: The chapter still opens on the exact interrogation beat where chapter1 ended; no recap drift was reintroduced.
Jaina's frost-burned hand, confiscated notes, confiscated map, and exhausted custody state remain callbacks rather than fresh reveals.
Sylvanas stays a security problem first and a person second; the chapter does not thaw the relationship past the plan's allowed crack.
Lyriel, Neral, Cyndia, and the unnamed escort remain in place for the chapter3 handoff exactly as required.
No grain answer, organized Cult infrastructure, or warm trust is smuggled in ahead of schedule.
observations: Required memory files cross-checked: jaina--sylvanas, antonidas--jaina, thalassian-pass-border-post, thalassian-pass, leyline-warning, jaina-proudmoore, sylvanas-windrunner.
The border post still reads as stone, ward-light, custody, confiscation, and controlled alertness in line with chapter1 memory.
Antonidas remains an off-page authority and liability, not a fresh exposition dump.
Jaina's belief that being right should be enough is still intact; the chapter only cracks it.
Sylvanas's competence is revised in Jaina's mind, not discovered from nowhere.

examples: Interrogation exposition compressed into plainer beats so the medium-run around Jaina's explanation does not turn metronomic.
Millhaven theory restatement shortened and re-angled to break a dense explanatory run.
Deer-diagnosis paragraph revised so the cadence alternates blunt findings with one longer explanatory sentence.
observations: I checked deliberate clipped runs at the opening commands, Jaina's necromancy naming, the custody terms, the command-to-motion beat, and the attack reports.
Many short runs are intentional because they are interrogative or tactical pressure, not accidental telegram prose.
The road-read section benefits from a few medium-length sentences to keep Jaina's thought chains from becoming all drumbeat.
Action reporting during the wolf/deer attack remains sharp because the slightly longer reorientation lines are spaced between impacts.
The chapter closes on longer atmospheric sentences before the final short hook, which preserves the approach-to-Millhaven rhythm.

examples: The deer-diagnosis aftermath now lands in a more immediate working silence, strengthening the bridge from fight to onward motion instead of letting the scene flatten abstractly.
continuity.crossChapter: Chapter 2 opens in direct carry from chapter1 and still preserves the same-moment seam.
observations: I checked the chapter bridge, ward-circle drop, exit from the pass, ground-reading stop, farm-evidence sequence, wildlife attack, and village approach.
No scene break markers interrupt continuous action where the plan calls for carryover motion.
The opening acknowledges chapter1's ending state through Jaina's exhaustion, confiscated papers, and immediate need to be heard.
The road progression stays causal: release leads to reading, reading leads to urgency, urgency leads to Millhaven, and Millhaven is interrupted by infected wildlife.
The chapter still ends on the same mounted party approaching the village proper, preserving chapter3's right to own the entry.

examples: Removed extra comparison scaffolding in Jaina's self-judgment so the prose commits faster.
Converted one plea-like internal line into a cleaner statement to cut leftover explanatory softness.
Trimmed filter-distance in the escort-read passage.
Reduced abstract phrasing in the aftermath silence so the scene lands in body and work rather than commentary.
observations: I checked opener tics, filter words, leftover document phrasing, simile density, and abstract-noun movement.
Purposeful comparisons remain where they sharpen contamination or emotional residue; weak comparison scaffolds were cut.
No biography insertions or re-expository clauses survived the pass.
The chapter's one-line dread beats still work because they follow concrete observation rather than explanation.
The dry humor moments survive because they are attached to pressure, not floating above it.

examples: Interrogation: 'It is preserving itself through transit' -> 'It's holding together as it moves.'
Interrogation: 'older magical structure' kept but reframed inside plainer spoken syntax.
Sylvanas: 'surviving the experiment' -> 'surviving the road.'
Millhaven theory: 'The local lattice narrows' -> 'Three local lines pinch together.'
Carcass diagnosis: 'teaching living flesh to answer necromancy before death has fully taken hold' -> plainer field speech built around what the deer did.
Carcass diagnosis: 'the body stops obeying ordinary limits cleanly' -> 'the body stops dying the way it should.'
observations: I scanned every quoted line, not just Jaina's, for report-language, lab phrasing, and academic over-formality.
Jaina is allowed one early over-technical slip because the plan and voice sheet require Sylvanas to correct her register in public.
After that correction, Jaina's speech now stays sharp without sounding like a paper read aloud.
Sylvanas's spoken lines remain short, finished, and a little mean; none were allowed to drift into bureaucratic cleverness.
Cyndia's speech stays practical and brief, consistent with supporting-cast guidance and field-role function.
